message.content:
You are researching Vibe Companion (the-vibe-companion) and Claude Code CLI integration.

CONTEXT:
- Project: Monitoring Claude Code CLI sessions via Vibe Companion dashboard
- Installation via Bun package manager
- Need: WebSocket integration, dashboard setup, monitoring capabilities

RESEARCH REQUIREMENTS:
1. Official Vibe Companion documentation and repository
2. Installation instructions with Bun
3. WebSocket protocol for connecting to Claude Code CLI
4. Dashboard features and how to use it
5. How Vibe Companion shows Claude Code sessions/tool calls
6. Port configuration (default: 3456)

SEARCH TERMS:
- "the-vibe-companion documentation 2026"
- "Vibe Companion Bun installation"
- "Vibe Companion Claude Code CLI integration"
- "the-vibe-companion websocket protocol"
- "Claude Code CLI dashboard monitoring"

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Return a markdown report with:
## Official Documentation
- Repository URL
- Documentation site
- Installation guide

## Installation & Setup
```bash
# Exact commands to install and run
bunx the-vibe-companion
```

## WebSocket Integration
```python
# How to connect to Vibe Companion WebSocket
# Message protocol format
# Session monitoring
```

## Dashboard Features
- What the dashboard shows
- How to view Claude Code sessions
- Real-time monitoring capabilities

## Critical Setup Notes
- Port configuration
- Required permissions
- Troubleshooting common issues

Based on the search results, I found several relevant resources about the Vibe Companion project and its integration with Claude Code CLI via WebSocket:

## Key Findings:

### 1. **The-Vibe-Company/companion (GitHub)**
The main repository appears to be `The-Vibe-Company/companion` on GitHub. The description mentions:
> "Claude Code CLI has a hidden --sdk-url flag that makes it connect to an external WebSocket server instead of running in a terminal."

This suggests the companion tool leverages a WebSocket connection to integrate with Claude Code CLI through the `--sdk-url` flag.

### 2. **Related Projects in the Ecosystem:**
- **Vibe Kanban** - An orchestration platform for AI coding agents that supports Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Cursor CLI, and others
- **Vibe Pocket** - A cloud-based platform for running CLI AI agents like Claude Code on mobile or web
- **agent-browser** - Vercel's CLI tool that works with Claude Code (mentioned on Reddit)

### 3. **Additional Resources:**
- There's a Chinese technical blog post analyzing Claude Code UI's WebSocket communication mechanism
- YouTube content about "Vibe Coding with Claude Code"
- Reddit discussions about various tools in the Vibe ecosystem

## Main Integration Point:
The core technology enabling this integration is the **hidden `--sdk-url` flag** in Claude Code CLI, which allows it to connect to external WebSocket servers rather than running in a standard terminal interface. This enables building custom UIs and integrations that communicate with Claude Code in real-time.

content.content:
[{'text': {'title': 'GitHub - The-Vibe-Company/companion: Web UI for Claude Code built on a reverse-engineered WebSocket protocol. Launch sessions, stream responses, approve tools — all from your browser.', 'description': 'Web UI for Claude Code built on a reverse-engineered WebSocket protocol. Launch sessions, stream responses, approve tools — all from your browser. - The-Vibe-Company/companion', 'url': 'https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion', 'content': "![Image 1: The Vibe Companion](https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion/raw/main/screenshot.png)

## The Vibe Companion

__An open-source web interface for Claude Code, built on an undocumented WebSocket protocol we reverse-engineered from the CLI.__

![Image 2: npm version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/50a5c24bc4b53dc3af6c96dd700966fecad5477b2428ea5c9aedae3536858c06/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f762f7468652d766962652d636f6d70616e696f6e2e737667)
![Image 3: npm downloads](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/ad3539cc147706378ba1d11f6573780d3d110824a5df92459791111e514df22b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f646d2f7468652d766962652d636f6d70616e696f6e2e737667)
![Image 4: MIT License](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/08cef40a9105b6526ca22088bc514fbfdbc9aac1ddbf8d4e6c750e3a88a44dca/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f4c6963656e73652d4d49542d626c75652e737667)
![Image 5: GitHub Stars](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/da31f08e22429d653269d81b89b4dec965cf6ed32fca8638585869e1b908a16a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f5468652d566962652d436f6d70616e792f636f6d70616e696f6e2e7376673f7374796c653d736f6369616c)

  
> Launch Claude Code sessions from your browser. Stream responses in real-time. Approve tool calls. Monitor multiple agents. No API key needed — uses your existing Claude Code subscription.

## Quick Start

> __Prerequisite:__ Claude Code CLI installed and authenticated.

That's it. Open http://localhost:3456 and start coding.

  

---

## Features

__Multi-session management__ — Launch multiple Claude Code sessions and switch between them. Each session runs in its own process with independent state, model, and permission settings.

__Real-time streaming__ — Watch responses generate token by token over WebSocket. See elapsed time and output token count as the agent works.

__Tool call visualization__ — Every tool call (Bash, Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep, WebSearch...) is displayed in collapsible blocks with syntax-highlighted content. See exactly what your agent is doing.

__Interactive permission control__ — Approve, deny, or edit tool inputs before they execute. Choose your permission mode per session: bypass all, accept edits, plan-only, or full manual control.

__Subagent task nesting__ — When agents spawn sub-agents via the Task tool, their work renders nested under the parent with collapsed previews and a dedicated task panel.

__Live session stats__ — Track cost (USD), context window usage, and turn count in real-time. A color-coded progress bar warns you as context fills up.

__Slash commands & image attachments__ — Use `/` to access commands and skills with autocomplete. Paste or upload images directly into your messages.

__Dark mode__ — Toggle between light and dark themes. Your preference persists across sessions.

  

---

## How It Works

Claude Code CLI has a __hidden `--sdk-url` flag__ that makes it connect to an external WebSocket server instead of running in a terminal. We reverse-engineered the NDJSON protocol it speaks and built a web server that bridges it to your browser.

```
                     WebSocket (NDJSON)              WebSocket (JSON)
┌──────────────┐    /ws/cli/:session         ┌─────────────────┐    /ws/browser/:session    ┌─────────────┐
│  Claude Code │ ◄──────────────────────────► │   Bun + Hono    │ ◄────────────────────────► │   Browser   │
│     CLI      │                              │     Server      │                            │  (React)    │
└──────────────┘                              └─────────────────┘                            └─────────────┘
                                               │
                                               ├─ Spawns CLI processes
                                               ├─ Routes messages bidirectionally
                                               ├─ Manages permission flow
                                               └─ Tracks session state & history
```

__The flow:__

1. You type a prompt in the browser
2. The server spawns `claude --sdk-url ws://localhost:3456/ws/cli/SESSION_ID --print --output-format stream-json`
3. The CLI connects back to the server over WebSocket
4. Messages flow both ways: your prompts go to the CLI, streaming responses come back to your browser
5. When the CLI wants to run a tool, it sends a permission request — the server forwards it to your browser for approval

  

---

## The Protocol

This project exists because we found something interesting buried in the Claude Code CLI binary: a hidden `--sdk-url` flag (`.hideHelp()` in Commander) that switches the CLI from terminal mode to WebSocket client mode.

The protocol it speaks is __NDJSON__ (newline-delimited JSON) — the same format used internally by the official `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. We reverse-engineered the full specification:

- __13 control request subtypes__ (initialize, can_use_tool, interrupt, set_model, MCP operations, and more)
- __Permission flow__ for tool approval/denial with input editing
- __Streaming events__ for token-by-token response delivery
- __Session lifecycle__ management and reconnection logic

The complete protocol specification is documented in `WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_REVERSED.md`.

  

---

## Development

```
git clone https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion.git
cd companion/web
bun install
bun run dev          # server on :3456 + Vite on :5174
```

Open http://localhost:5174 for hot-reloading development.

For production builds:

```
bun run build && bun run start   # everything on :3456
```

  

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
| --- | --- |
| Runtime | Bun |
| Server | Hono + native Bun WebSocket |
| Frontend | React 19 + TypeScript |
| State | Zustand |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Build | Vite |

  

---

## Roadmap

The Vibe Companion started as a way to control Claude Code from the browser. But the vision is bigger:

__An open-source web interface for AI coding agents, compatible with any LLM provider.__

What's coming:

- Support for additional LLM providers beyond Anthropic
- Bring-your-own-API-key mode
- Collaborative multi-user sessions
- Plugin system for custom tool integrations
- Self-hosted deployment with Docker

If you want to help shape the future of AI-assisted development, contributions are welcome.

  

---

## License

Launch sessions, stream responses, approve tools — all from your browser.", "description": "Web UI for Claude Code built on a reverse-engineered WebSocket protocol. Launch sessions, stream responses, approve tools — all from your browser. - The-Vibe-Company/companion", "url": "https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion", "content": "![Image 1: The Vibe Companion](https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion/raw/main/screenshot.png)

## The Vibe Companion

__An open-source web interface for Claude Code, built on an undocumented WebSocket protocol we reverse-engineered from the CLI.__

![Image 2: npm version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/50a5c24bc4b53dc3af6c96dd700966fecad5477b2428ea5c9aedae3536858c06/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f762f7468652d766962652d636f6d70616e696f6e2e737667)
![Image 3: npm downloads](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/ad3539cc147706378ba1d11f6573780d3d110824a5df92459791111e514df22b/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6e706d2f646d2f7468652d766962652d636f6d70616e696f6e2e737667)
![Image 4: MIT License](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/08cef40a9105b6526ca22088bc514fbfdbc9aac1ddbf8d4e6c750e3a88a44dca/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f4c6963656e73652d4d49542d626c75652e737667)
![Image 5: GitHub Stars](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/da31f08e22429d653269d81b89b4dec965cf6ed32fca8638585869e1b908a16a/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f73746172732f5468652d566962652d436f6d70616e792f636f6d70616e696f6e2e7376673f7374796c653d736f6369616c)

  
> Launch Claude Code sessions from your browser. Stream responses in real-time. Approve tool calls. Monitor multiple agents. No API key needed — uses your existing Claude Code subscription.

## Quick Start

> __Prerequisite:__ Claude Code CLI installed and authenticated.

That's it. Open http://localhost:3456 and start coding.

  

---

## Features

__Multi-session management__ — Launch multiple Claude Code sessions and switch between them. Each session runs in its own process with independent state, model, and permission settings.

__Real-time streaming__ — Watch responses generate token by token over WebSocket. See elapsed time and output token count as the agent works.

__Tool call visualization__ — Every tool call (Bash, Read, Edit, Write, Glob, Grep, WebSearch...) is displayed in collapsible blocks with syntax-highlighted content. See exactly what your agent is doing.

__Interactive permission control__ — Approve, deny, or edit tool inputs before they execute. Choose your permission mode per session: bypass all, accept edits, plan-only, or full manual control.

__Subagent task nesting__ — When agents spawn sub-agents via the Task tool, their work renders nested under the parent with collapsed previews and a dedicated task panel.

__Live session stats__ — Track cost (USD), context window usage, and turn count in real-time. A color-coded progress bar warns you as context fills up.

__Slash commands & image attachments__ — Use `/` to access commands and skills with autocomplete. Paste or upload images directly into your messages.

__Dark mode__ — Toggle between light and dark themes. Your preference persists across sessions.

  

---

## How It Works

Claude Code CLI has a __hidden `--sdk-url` flag__ that makes it connect to an external WebSocket server instead of running in a terminal. We reverse-engineered the NDJSON protocol it speaks and built a web server that bridges it to your browser.

```
                     WebSocket (NDJSON)              WebSocket (JSON)
┌──────────────┐    /ws/cli/:session         ┌─────────────────┐    /ws/browser/:session    ┌─────────────┐
│  Claude Code │ ◄──────────────────────────► │   Bun + Hono    │ ◄────────────────────────► │   Browser   │
│     CLI      │                              │     Server      │                            │  (React)    │
└──────────────┘                              └─────────────────┘                            └─────────────┘
                                               │
                                               ├─ Spawns CLI processes
                                               ├─ Routes messages bidirectionally
                                               ├─ Manages permission flow
                                               └─ Tracks session state & history
```

__The flow:__

1. You type a prompt in the browser
2. The server spawns `claude --sdk-url ws://localhost:3456/ws/cli/SESSION_ID --print --output-format stream-json`
3. The CLI connects back to the server over WebSocket
4. Messages flow both ways: your prompts go to the CLI, streaming responses come back to your browser
5. When the CLI wants to run a tool, it sends a permission request — the server forwards it to your browser for approval

  

---

## The Protocol

This project exists because we found something interesting buried in the Claude Code CLI binary: a hidden `--sdk-url` flag (`.hideHelp()` in Commander) that switches the CLI from terminal mode to WebSocket client mode.

The protocol it speaks is __NDJSON__ (newline-delimited JSON) — the same format used internally by the official `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`. We reverse-engineered the full specification:

- __13 control request subtypes__ (initialize, can_use_tool, interrupt, set_model, MCP operations, and more)
- __Permission flow__ for tool approval/denial with input editing
- __Streaming events__ for token-by-token response delivery
- __Session lifecycle__ management and reconnection logic

The complete protocol specification is documented in `WEBSOCKET_PROTOCOL_REVERSED.md`.

  

---

## Development

```
git clone https://github.com/The-Vibe-Company/companion.git
cd companion/web
bun install
bun run dev          # server on :3456 + Vite on :5174
```

Open http://localhost:5174 for hot-reloading development.

For production builds:

```
bun run build && bun run start   # everything on :3456
```

  

---

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
| --- | --- |
| Runtime | Bun |
| Server | Hono + native Bun WebSocket |
| Frontend | React 19 + TypeScript |
| State | Zustand |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Build | Vite |

  

---

## Roadmap

The Vibe Companion started as a way to control Claude Code from the browser. But the vision is bigger:

__An open-source web interface for AI coding agents, compatible with any LLM provider.__

What's coming:

- Support for additional LLM providers beyond Anthropic
- Bring-your-own-API-key mode
- Collaborative multi-user sessions
- Plugin system for custom tool integrations
- Self-hosted deployment with Docker

If you want to help shape the future of AI-assisted development, contributions are welcome.

  

---

## License
